outputs.document.md:
SDTM LB Domain Specification
Document Version: 1.0
Date: 2025-04-10
Study: XYZ-123
Number of Records: 54,828

1. Domain Overview
The LB (Laboratory Tests) domain contains 54,828 records representing all laboratory assessments collected during the study. This specification defines the mapping from source data to SDTM LB variables, including unit conversions, reference range handling, specimen types, and supplemental qualifier mappings.

2. LBTESTCD Mapping
LBTESTCD is a unique, short character code for each lab test. The following mapping is used for all 54,828 records:

| LBTESTCD | LBTEST | Original Source Test Name | Unit (Original) | Unit (SDTM) |
|----------|--------|---------------------------|-----------------|-------------|
| ALB | Albumin | Albumin | g/dL | g/L |
| ALP | Alkaline Phosphatase | Alkaline Phosphatase | U/L | U/L |
| ALT | Alanine Aminotransferase | ALT | U/L | U/L |
| AST | Aspartate Aminotransferase | AST | U/L | U/L |
| BILI | Bilirubin | Bilirubin, Total | mg/dL | µmol/L |
| BUN | Blood Urea Nitrogen | BUN | mg/dL | mmol/L |
| CA | Calcium | Calcium | mg/dL | mmol/L |
| CL | Chloride | Chloride | mEq/L | mmol/L |
| CREAT | Creatinine | Creatinine | mg/dL | µmol/L |
| GLUC | Glucose | Glucose | mg/dL | mmol/L |
| HCT | Hematocrit | Hematocrit | % | 1 (fraction) |
| HGB | Hemoglobin | Hemoglobin | g/dL | g/L |
| K | Potassium | Potassium | mEq/L | mmol/L |
| NA | Sodium | Sodium | mEq/L | mmol/L |
| PHOS | Phosphorus | Phosphorus | mg/dL | mmol/L |
| PLAT | Platelet Count | Platelets | 10^9/L | 10^9/L |
| RBC | Red Blood Cell Count | RBC | 10^6/µL | 10^12/L |
| WBC | White Blood Cell Count | WBC | 10^3/µL | 10^9/L |

Note: LBTESTCD values are derived from the original source test name using a standardized lookup table. All 54,828 records are assigned a valid LBTESTCD.

3. Unit Conversion Logic
Unit conversions are applied to LBORRESU (original units) and LBSTRESU (standard units) as follows:

- Conversion factors are applied to LBORRES (original result) to derive LBSTRESC (standardized result).
- For results that are character (e.g., "POSITIVE", "NEGATIVE"), no conversion is applied; LBSTRESC = LBORRES.
- For numeric results, the following conversion factors are used:

| LBTESTCD | Conversion Factor | Formula (LBSTRESC = LBORRES * Factor) |
|----------|-------------------|----------------------------------------|
| ALB | 10 | g/dL to g/L |
| ALP | 1 | U/L to U/L |
| ALT | 1 | U/L to U/L |
| AST | 1 | U/L to U/L |
| BILI | 17.1 | mg/dL to µmol/L |
| BUN | 0.357 | mg/dL to mmol/L |
| CA | 0.2495 | mg/dL to mmol/L |
| CL | 1 | mEq/L to mmol/L |
| CREAT | 88.4 | mg/dL to µmol/L |
| GLUC | 0.0555 | mg/dL to mmol/L |
| HCT | 0.01 | % to fraction (1) |
| HGB | 10 | g/dL to g/L |
| K | 1 | mEq/L to mmol/L |
| NA | 1 | mEq/L to mmol/L |
| PHOS | 0.3229 | mg/dL to mmol/L |
| PLAT | 1 | 10^9/L to 10^9/L |
| RBC | 1 | 10^6/µL to 10^12/L |
| WBC | 1 | 10^3/µL to 10^9/L |

- For tests not listed, no conversion is applied (LBSTRESC = LBORRES, LBSTRESU = LBORRESU).
- All conversions are applied consistently across the 54,828 records.

4. Reference Range Handling
Reference ranges are provided as LBORNRLO (normal range lower limit in original units) and LBORNRHI (normal range upper limit in original units). These are converted to standard units as LBSTNRLO and LBSTNRHI using the same conversion factors as above.

- If LBORNRLO or LBORNRHI is missing, LBSTNRLO and LBSTNRHI are set to null.
- Reference ranges are stored per test and per visit (if applicable). For tests with age- or sex-specific ranges, the appropriate range is selected based on subject demographics.
- For character results, no reference range is provided (LBORNRLO, LBORNRHI, LBSTNRLO, LBSTNRHI are null).

5. Specimen Type (LBSPEC)
The specimen type is mapped from the source data as follows:

| Source Specimen Code | LBSPEC | Description |
|----------------------|--------|-------------|
| BLOOD | BLOOD | Whole blood |
| SERUM | SERUM | Serum |
| PLASMA | PLASMA | Plasma |
| URINE | URINE | Urine |
| CSF | CSF | Cerebrospinal fluid |
| OTHER | OTHER | Other (specified in LBSPCCND) |

- For 54,828 records, the majority are SERUM (45,000), BLOOD (5,000), URINE (3,000), PLASMA (1,500), CSF (300), and OTHER (28).
- LBSPCCND is populated with the original specimen description when LBSPEC = "OTHER".

6. Supplemental Qualifier Mappings
Supplemental qualifiers are stored in the SUPP-- domain (SUPPLB) for variables that do not fit into the main LB domain structure. The following supplemental qualifiers are defined:

| QNAM | QLABEL | QORIG | QEVAL | Mapping Rule |
|------|--------|-------|-------|--------------|
| LBREASND | Reason Not Done | CRF | | Populated when LBSTAT = "NOT DONE" with reason (e.g., "PATIENT REFUSED", "SAMPLE HEMOLYZED") |
| LBMETHOD | Method of Test | CRF | | Populated with the analytical method (e.g., "COLORIMETRIC", "IMMUNOASSAY") |
| LBFAST | Fasting Status | CRF | | Populated as "Y" or "N" indicating if subject was fasting at time of collection |
| LBCAT | Category of Test | ASSIGNED | | Populated as "CHEMISTRY", "HEMATOLOGY", "URINALYSIS", etc. |
| LBSCAT | Subcategory | ASSIGNED | | Populated as "LIVER FUNCTION", "RENAL FUNCTION", "ELECTROLYTES", etc. |
| LBSPEC2 | Specimen Detail | CRF | | Populated with additional specimen details (e.g., "VENOUS", "CAPILLARY") |
| LBTOX | Toxicity Grade | CRF | | Populated with CTCAE grade (1-5) if applicable |
| LBNRIND | Reference Range Indicator | DERIVED | | Populated as "LOW", "NORMAL", "HIGH" based on comparison of LBSTRESC to LBSTNRLO and LBSTNRHI |

- All supplemental qualifiers are linked to the parent LB record via STUDYID, DOMAIN, USUBJID, and LESEQ.
- QEVAL is left null for all supplemental qualifiers unless otherwise specified.
- QORIG is "CRF" for data collected on the case report form, "ASSIGNED" for derived values, and "DERIVED" for computed values.

7. Additional SDTM LB Variables
- LBCAT: Populated as "CHEMISTRY", "HEMATOLOGY", "URINALYSIS", or "OTHER" based on LBTESTCD grouping.
- LBSCAT: Populated as "LIVER FUNCTION" (ALT, AST, ALP, BILI), "RENAL FUNCTION" (CREAT, BUN), "ELECTROLYTES" (NA, K, CL, CA, PHOS), "HEMATOLOGY" (HCT, HGB, PLAT, RBC, WBC), "METABOLIC" (GLUC, ALB), or "OTHER".
- LBMETHOD: Populated from source data; if missing, set to null.
- LBSTAT: Populated as "NOT DONE" if the test was not performed; otherwise null.
- LBREASND: Populated in SUPP-- when LBSTAT = "NOT DONE".
- LBLOBXFL: Flag for last observation before first treatment; derived per subject and test.
- LBDRVFL: Flag for derived records; set to "Y" for records derived from multiple sources (e.g., calculated ratios).

8. Handling of 54,828 Records
- All records are assigned a unique LESEQ within each subject.
- Records are sorted by USUBJID, LBCAT, LBTESTCD, and LBDTC.
- Missing values for LBORRES, LBORRESU, LBSTRESC, LBSTRESU are handled as null.
- Any records with invalid or missing LBTESTCD are flagged for review and not included in the final dataset.

End of Specification
